item.title: EPA to open habitats of endangered species to logging and mining
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: endangered species — Habitat destruction strongest driver of species loss, with legislation keeping 99% of listed species from going extinctThe Trump administration repealed a crucial part of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) on Friday, finalizing a new rule that will open habitats of imperiled wildlife to development, logging, mining and other uses.For the last 50 years, the landmark environmental law included a broader understanding of the word “harm”, which ensured that not just the plants and animals themselves w

item.title: The UT San Antonio robotics team behind RoboRowdy wins global competition
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The all-freshman, multidisciplinary team, had been named the sole finalist to represent the U.S. in the global engineering competition. The UT San Antonio robotics team behind RoboRowdy wins global competition was first posted on July 10, 2026 at 3:48 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: Moderate warming rewires one-third of microalga's genes, study finds
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Researchers have demonstrated for the first time that the microalga Chlamydomonas reinhardtii alters the activity of about one-third of its protein-coding genes in response even to moderate temperature changes. The study, published in the journal The Plant Cell, points to far-reaching consequences of climate change for aquatic ecosystems and soil. The research team is from the Cluster of Excellence Balance of the Microverse at Friedrich Schiller University Jena and the Leibniz Institute for Natu

item.title: DHS’s Account of Salgado Araujo Killing Is “A Lie,” Witness Says
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: immigration detention — All three people in the car with Salgado Araujo are now in immigration detention and facing deportation proceedings.

item.title: Brazil deforestation hits new low in Amazon
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: deforestation — Deforestation in the Brazilian Amazon fell to its lowest level in a decade in the first half of the year, according to official figures released Friday.

item.title: The Freedom of Information Act Is in Serious Trouble Under Trump
item.description: [Government Accountability] Keyword: FOIA — Delay and deferral tactics threaten accountability, transparency, and democratic processes, says a FOIA advocate.

item.title: Math reveals how honeybee hives balance the 'daring few, patient many' strategy
item.description: [Foraging & Wild Plants] Keyword: foraging — How do bees make group decisions without a leader? Math experts have determined that the best strategy is for a few to assume the risk of foraging under all conditions while the majority stay safely back and forage only when conditions are favorable.

item.title: Destructive Windows backdoor stuffs multiple wipers and ransomware code into a single package
item.description: [Cybersecurity] Keyword: ransomware — Microsoft says GigaWiper combines at least 3 malware families into one modular tool

item.title: FOIA Under Attack: Landmark Transparency Law Turns 60; Fed Gov't Blocking More Documents Than Ever
item.description: [Government Accountability] Keyword: FOIA — This month marks the 60th anniversary of the Freedom of Information Act, the landmark government transparency law that has helped reveal and publicize critical information about everything from the Vietnam War to FBI surveillance to CIA torture. For decades, FOIA has played a crucial role in uncovering and rectifying government wrongdoing. Today, however, advocates say that the government’s resistance to fulfilling FOIA requests has grown, forcing applicants to file expensive lawsuits to o

item.title: As Texas governor’s race tightens, supporters rally behind Hinojosa in San Antonio
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Supporters packed the Paper Tiger on Thursday evening, waving "Money in Your Pocket" signs as Gina Hinojosa campaigned for governor.As Texas governor’s race tightens, supporters rally behind Hinojosa in San Antonio was first posted on July 10, 2026 at 10:14 am.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. Please contact me at hel

item.title: [$] An update on the scraper situation
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: large language model — Our article "Fighting the AI scraper bot scourge", published in early 2025, discussed the problem of widespread scraping of web sites in search of training data for large language models and related projects. This activity overwhelms sites with traffic. Over a year after that article is published, the problem is still growing. The hammering of sites by shadowy actors has reached new heights, and the open web is becoming increasingly difficult to maintain. Where is this traffic coming from, a

item.title: The Trump Administration Is Overhauling Birth Control Access for the Pronatalist Movement
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — Under RFK Jr., HHS is revamping a program that’s supposed to fund contraceptives and sexual healthcare to please a far-right movement with roots in eugenics. The post The Trump Administration Is Overhauling Birth Control Access for the Pronatalist Movement appeared first on The Intercept.

item.title: Why Pastoral Production Requires Regional Coordination, Harmonised Policy
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — At the 64th sessions of the Subsidiary Bodies (SB64) under the UNFCCC in Bonn, Germany, the Local Communities and Indigenous Peoples Platform (LCIPP) underscored the importance of ethically and equitably incorporating indigenous values and knowledge and local knowledge systems such as pastoralism into climate policies and actions ahead of the 31st Conference of Parties on […]

item.title: Pacific gray whales facing ‘catastrophic’ die-off as climate crisis hits food supply
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Trump administration urged to relist a species in ‘very, very serious trouble’ under Endangered Species ActClimate change is driving a gray whale “catastrophic mortality event” in the Pacific Ocean as melting sea ice depletes food sources and the animals starve, environmental groups warn.Meanwhile, a range of other issues, like ship strikes, oil spills, microplastic pollution, algal blooms and Russian harvesting are also probably contributing to the die-off that has nearly halved the whales’ es

item.title: Nature’s Ingenious Survival Strategies Are No Match for Human Destruction
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: endangered species — This story was originally published by the Guardian and is reproduced here as part of the Climate Desk collaboration. Life has colonized every corner of the planet by evolving ingenious survival strategies, but these are increasingly being overwhelmed by destructive human activities, this year’s red list of endangered species has revealed. Many snails, limpets and clams have adapted to life at […]

item.title: Yonder Radio: Sale Barns, Regenerative Farming, and Midwestern Punk Rock
item.description: [Small Farm & Urban Agriculture] Keyword: regenerative agriculture — Tune in for conversations about the social value of sale barns, regenerative agriculture in the corn belt, the Rural Health Transformation Program, and punk rock in Minot, North Dakota. Plus, music from Trevor McKenzie. The post Yonder Radio: Sale Barns, Regenerative Farming, and Midwestern Punk Rock appeared first on The Daily Yonder.

item.title: US pushes for weaker truck pollution rules
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — President Donald Trump's administration on Thursday moved to loosen truck pollution regulations put in place by his predecessor, Joe Biden, in its latest environmental rollback benefiting makers of fossil fuel-burning vehicles.

item.title: ‘He’s forcing higher bills’: Trump spends billions to kill clean energy and keep coal alive
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Critics accuse president of ‘fattening the wallets of his cronies’ as working Americans face higher energy ratesThe Trump administration has directly spent $2.7bn of taxpayer money on its crusade against wind power while pouring $1.125bn into boosting coal, which critics say is pushing up Americans’ bills.They say the moves are evidence that the president aims to serve fossil-fuel companies like those which donated record sums to his presidential campaign, rather than the working-class Americans

item.title: One U.S. visa program is growing rapidly. No one is happy with it
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: visa program — Republicans on Capitol Hill are starting to talk about one facet of immigration reform: how to expand the popular H-2A visa program for farm laborers. They face obstacles.

item.title: Esquire has a new owner, a former bartender who loved the bar ‘from the moment I walked through the front door’

item.title: Germany: Bundestag passes controversial healthcare reform
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — The cost-cutting package passed Germany's lower house, with protests against it expected to take place. Elsewhere, Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul will meet his Slovenian counterpart in Berlin. Follow DW for more.

item.title: Taliban declares war on smartphones
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — A newly announced ban on smartphones for government workers, police and military personnel is spilling over into healthcare and educational facilities. Ordinary citizens worry they'll be next.

item.title: Anthony Albanese will not attend Garma festival, despite vowing to attend every year as prime minister
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — Albanese has attended the Indigenous cultural festival every year since 2019 and committed to ‘be here and engaged with you’ each yeah he remains in officeFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastAnthony Albanese won’t attend this month’s Garma festival, breaking a commitment made just 12 months ago to travel to north-east Arnhem Land each year for Australia’s largest Indigenous cultural gathering.The prime minister last yea

item.title: Morning glories reveal 96% drop in adaptation as pollinator pressure reshapes evolution
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Facing both climate change and a crashing pollinator population, plants may be evolving to attract pollinators rather than adapting to a warming climate, and the trade-off has resulted in a steep decline in plants' rate of adaptation, according to a University of Michigan study. The researchers, studying morning glories, observed a 96% decrease in the population's rate of adaptation over nine years. The declining rate of adaptation could affect farmers, who deal with morning glory as an agricult

item.title: Q&A: Examining the Global South's call for environmental justice amid expanding technology
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: environmental justice — Digital technologies—from artificial intelligence to smartphones and data centers—are often described as "clean" innovations. Yet every device depends on minerals, electricity, labor and global supply chains, raising important questions about environmental justice and development.

item.title: Scientists found a longevity diet that helped mice eat more and lose fat
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: type 2 diabetes — Scientists found that a modified Mediterranean-style diet with low protein and just enough methionine helped mice live healthier lives while reducing body fat and frailty. Human data also linked lower animal protein intake to lower rates of obesity and Type 2 diabetes, suggesting the approach could benefit people as well.

item.title: WSJ just gave Trump a big economic reality check
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — President Donald Trump frequently claims that his tariffs are saving America’s economy, but a recent opinion piece from The Wall Street Journal Editorial Board just gave him a big reality check.The Journal opened by mentioning that Toyota recently announced plans to invest $3.6 billion in a second assembly line at a San Antonio facility to produce its Tacoma trucks, creating 2,000 jobs in the process. Trump claimed that this vindicates his tariff system, writing on social media that “Toyota is m

item.title: Report on Elevated Lung Cancer Fails to Quell Uproar Around New York’s Largest Landfill
item.description: [Stoicism & Philosophy] Keyword: Seneca — This article previously appeared in Water Front.  SENECA FALLS, N.Y.—In its recent 20-page report on elevated lung cancer rates around Seneca Meadows Inc., the state Department of Health (DOH) officially declares that the state’s largest municipal waste landfill deserves no blame. That conclusion has hardly soothed those who have long complained of sickening odors and respiratory ailments that have […]

item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Excess deaths in France, Belgium, Spain, and the Netherlands last month exceeded 4,600.

item.title: Data Centers Are Building Power Plants Near Neighborhoods—That May Play Into Some South San Antonio Ambitions
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — While some Texas cities have advanced bans and moratoriums on data centers until the risks are better understood, San Antonio has proceeded more cautiously toward possible regulation. And some see economic opportunity.

item.title: "We Want Texans to Know Their Rights": Q&A with Mayday Health on the Impact of Surveillance on Abortion Care
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — Last May, EFF reported that a sheriff’s office in Texas searched data from more than 83,000 automated license plate reader (ALPR) cameras to track down a woman suspected of self-managing an abortion. ALPRs are promoted as tools for keeping communities safe by finding missing persons and locating stolen vehicles, but this case showed how ALPRS can be weaponized to investigate people’s private healthcare decisions. And these aren’t the only tools in the surveillance arsenal: others include locatio

item.title: Florida Regulators Stood by as Dozens of Sloths Died, New Records Reveal
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: wildlife conservation — The first warning reached the Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission before a single sloth had arrived in Orlando.  On Dec. 9, 2024, a member of the public called the agency after seeing advertisements for a new business called Sloth World. The caller said owner Benjamin Agresta was hiring employees and telling prospective workers that […]

item.title: Heat waves: Why British trees are shedding branches and dying
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — If you visit the Royal Botanic Gardens at Kew, on the edge of London, you will see a brightly painted skeleton of a dead oak tree. The tree, known as the climate-changed oak, succumbed in the heat wave of 2022. Instead of removing it, Kew left it in place as a reminder that climate change is already taking its toll on Britain's trees.

item.title: UK swelters in third heatwave of the year as western Europe counts cost of hottest-ever June
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Britain expands heat alerts while estimates suggest June’s death toll could surpass 20,000 across continentThe UK is sweltering through the peak of its third heatwave of the year as countries around Europe struggle to recover from an early onslaught of baking summer heat.Punishing temperatures pushed higher by fossil fuel pollution have broken records across the continent in recent weeks. Western Europe experienced its hottest June on record, scientists confirmed on Thursday, accompanied by high

item.title: Earthquakes in Venezuela expose a severely under-resourced and unprepared healthcare system
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — Two physicians discuss the ongoing earthquake relief efforts in their home country of Venezuela and explain what the healthcare system was like before the disaster.

item.title: The color of penguin poo: Satellites reveal global warming's impact on an iconic polar species
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: global warming — Scientists from a handful of universities across the country have made innovative use of satellite images from NASA to determine the diet of Antarctic Adélie penguins across the continent by studying their icy feces with a fidelity and frequency that could be captured only by space technology.

item.title: Fossils found decades ago reveal extinct 3.5 million-year-old giant salamander species
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — In the late 1990s in the Ajimu region of Japan's Oita Prefecture, researchers discovered three fossilized vertebrae belonging to the Cryptobranchidae family of giant salamanders. These were embedded in the Tsubusugawa Formation, Pliocene-era strata of lake deposits dating back approximately 3.5 million years. The strata have also yielded fossils of animals that no longer roam Japan, such as elephants and crocodiles, revealing a glimpse of an era much warmer and more humid than Japan's current cl

item.title: What Is (and Isn’t) Happening with the Border Wall in Big Bend
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: border wall — You’ve probably heard about the Trump administration’s plans for a border wall through Big Bend. Or its plans not to build a wall at all, but instead to put up a futuristic forcefield of lights and sensors. You may have even heard that public pressure backed the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) into a corner […] The post What Is (and Isn’t) Happening with the Border Wall in Big Bend appeared first on The Texas Observer.

item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — The Pentagon recategorized Native religions as ‘other.’ A scholar of Native American and Indigenous Studies explains the implications of that change.

item.title: Species’ ingenious survival strategies no match for human destruction, red list reveals
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: endangered species — Newly endangered animals include desert frogs and snails in extreme ocean depths, both threatened by miningLife has colonised every corner of the planet by evolving ingenious survival strategies but these are increasingly being overwhelmed by destructive human activities, this year’s red list of endangered species has revealed.Many snails, limpets and clams have adapted to life at crushing depths in the oceans on hydrothermal vents where water temperatures can reach 450C (842F). But an assessmen

item.title: This Mars rover could finally reveal whether life ever existed on Mars
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — The hunt for ancient life on Mars just got an important test run. Scientists confirmed that the Rosalind Franklin rover's sophisticated instrument can detect subtle differences in two stable molecules that could preserve evidence of past life for billions of years. But the team also uncovered a surprise: organic molecules in the Murchison meteorite appear to have been contaminated by fossil fuel pollution during their journey through Earth's atmosphere.

item.title: How to build a highway in the age of climate change
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change —

item.title: Ancient fossil may reveal animal kingdom's earliest right-handedness at 550 million years old
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Scientists have uncovered what may be the earliest evidence of "right-handedness" in the animal kingdom, dating back more than half a billion years. The discovery comes from the fossil record of Spriggina floundersi, an organism from the Ediacaran Period that lived about 550 million years ago.

item.title: The Iran war has pushed some countries away from oil and toward clean energy
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — The new round of bombing in the Middle East has underscored the precarity of relying on fossil fuels. The war is speeding up the global transition to EVs, solar, and batteries, experts say.

item.title: Organizer from the conservative Americans for Prosperity nominated to serve on appraisal board
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Jonathan "Jon" Melendez, a grassroots engagement director at Americans for Prosperity, was among 15 candidates who applied for a temporary appointment after BCAD board member Robert “Bob” Bruce died in March.Organizer from the conservative Americans for Prosperity nominated to serve on appraisal board was first posted on July 9, 2026 at 5:00 am.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: Rare goblin shark filmed alive for the first time in the deep sea
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — For the first time, researchers have filmed the elusive goblin shark alive in the deep ocean where it naturally lives. The remarkable sightings greatly expand the shark's known range and depth, showing that this 125-million-year-old "living fossil" still has plenty of secrets left to reveal.

item.title: [$] LWN.net Weekly Edition for July 9, 2026
item.description:
[AI & Automation] Keyword: LLM — Inside this week's LWN.net Weekly Edition:
Front: Cryptography API; Iomap explanation; Negative dentries; Faster RCUs and lockless allocation for BPF; Negative dentries; LLMs in memory-management code Briefs: Guix vulnerabilities; OpenSSH 10.4; trusted publishing; kernel archive; CalyxOS; Quotes; ... Announcements: Newsletters, conferences, security updates, patches, and more.

item.title: Confirmed Ebola deaths in DR Congo hit 600
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — The number of deaths comes as healthcare workers threaten to walk off the job over a delay in payments.

item.title: Decoding of one of nature's largest enzymes reveals electron flow behind biological methane production
item.description: [Science & Research] Keyword: microbiology — A research team at Marburg University has investigated one of the largest enzyme complexes found in nature to date and deciphered its remarkable structure. Under the supervision of Dr. Jan Schuller, Ph.D. student Sophia Paul from the Center for Synthetic Microbiology (SYNMIKRO) was able to characterize the so-called heterodisulfide reductase super-assembly in detail. The results of the study are now published in the journal Nature. They show how a molecular "giant" comprising hundreds of buildin

item.title: Indigenous peoples in the Amazon face massive cultural and ecological loss due to climate change
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — The Amazon region, Earth's most important ecosystem, is home to more than 400 Indigenous groups that use thousands of rainforest plant species. They pass on their knowledge of the flora primarily through oral tradition, usually from parents or other family members to their children. This creates a "living library of knowledge" about how to use native plants. Until now, little was known about how this treasure trove of knowledge is affected by the combined effects of climate change and language l

item.title: Woman accused of stealing ambulance while medics treated patient in downtown San Antonio
item.description: [Texas Surveillance] Keyword: San Antonio police — San Antonio police arrested a woman accused of stealing an ambulance while two paramedics treated a patient in the back. Woman accused of stealing ambulance while medics treated patient in downtown San Antonio was first posted on July 8, 2026 at 3:14 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". item.title: International team says science alone won't save coral reefs
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Coral reefs are disappearing at an unprecedented rate as climate change, marine heat waves, pollution and coastal development threaten one of Earth's richest ecosystems. While scientific research has greatly advanced understanding of the crisis and ways to restore damaged reefs, a new international paper argues that science alone will not be enough to protect them.

item.title: Assistant Chief Jesse Salame named acting San Antonio police chief
item.description: [Texas Surveillance] Keyword: San Antonio police — Jesus “Jesse” Salame Jr. will serve as acting San Antonio police chief as the city searches for a permanent replacement for William McManus.Assistant Chief Jesse Salame named acting San Antonio police chief was first posted on July 8, 2026 at 1:15 pm.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. item.title: How Fourth of July celebrations and the national political mood may shape psychedelic experiences
item.description: [Psychedelics & Psilocybin Research] Keyword: psychedelic medicine — Psychedelic drugs are known to make people highly sensitive to their surroundings. In other words, a user's mindset and immediate environment heavily shape the entire trippy experience. In a study published in the journal Psychedelic Medicine, scientists wanted to test a brand-new idea: whether an invisible backdrop of national culture, rather than just a person's local setting, could influence people's support for partisan violence after taking a psychedelic.
